message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualizaÃ§Ã£o e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

   292â†’# SP_0003.2 â€” Storybook JSON Editor Addon (IndexedDB Persistence)
   293â†’- [DONE] Create a custom Storybook addon panel that allows editing the SDUI JSON

   318â†’     - JsonEditorPanel.tsx (the editor UI component)

   970â†’# SP_0006.0 â€” JSON Logic Evaluator
   971â†’- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
   972â†’  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts â€” evaluateRule() function with:
   973â†’  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
   974â†’  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
   975â†’  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
   976â†’  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
   977â†’  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
   978â†’  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
   979â†’  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
   980â†’  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
   981â†’  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
   982â†’  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
   983â†’  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.
   984â†’
   985â†’Context:
   986â†’- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
   987â†’  and, or, ! (logical)
   988â†’- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
   989â†’  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
   990â†’- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
   991â†’- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
   992â†’- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras invÃ¡lidas
   993â†’  retornam false")
   994â†’- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
   995â†’  true (show component) â€” hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
   996â†’- Must be pure function, no side effects
   997â†’- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/
   998â†’
   999â†’Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
  1000â†’comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.
  1001â†’

- [DONE] SP_0XX0.5 - Setup Claude Code Playwright MCP.
  > Created .claude/settings.json with @playwright/mcp MCP server (headless).
  > Requires Claude Code session restart to activate MCP tools.
  > Playwright 1.58.2 + Chromium browsers already installed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.0 â€” Setup Storybook
- [DONE] Configure Storybook so this MFE can render screens independently of the
SuperApp.
  > Storybook 8.6.17 with @storybook/react-native-web-vite running on port 6006.
  > Custom Vite plugins: jsxInJsPlugin, dtsStubPlugin, circularFixPlugin, cjsToEsmPlugin.
  > ThemeProvider decorator in preview.tsx. Zustand store mocked in .storybook/mocks/store.ts.
  > SmokeTest story renders Text, Button, Box from DS successfully.

Context:
- This is a React Native MFE (package.json: react 18.2.0, react-native 0.73.6)
- The design system (@semparar/design-system-app ^3.39.15) already has Storybook
  configured â€” its setup can be used as reference if the executing agent needs it
- Babel aliases: ~ â†’ ./src/ (babel.config.js line 27)
- TS alias: ~/store â†’ ./@types/store.d.ts (tsconfig.json line 11) â€” this is a
  module augmentation for the SuperApp's Zustand store, needs mocking
- DS components depend on design tokens â€” may need a ThemeProvider or similar

Deliverable: `npm run storybook` opens a browser with a smoke test story that
renders at least one DS component (e.g., Button or Text from @semparar/design-system-app).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.1 â€” Validate DS components in Storybook
- [DONE] Prove that the design system components actually used in voucher screens
      render correctly in the Storybook web context.
  > 8/10 DS components render in Storybook web context.
  > BackDropBottom (Modalize) and Loading (Lottie) require native modules â€” cannot render on web.
  > SDUI adapters should provide web-safe fallbacks for these two.
  > Full validation story at src/__stories__/DSComponentValidation.stories.tsx.

Context:
- The voucher screens use these DS components: Text, Button, Box, InputBox,
  Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal, BackDropBottom, Loading
- See existing usage in:
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx (form with inputs, balance, button)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx (review, success, error states)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx (list items with icons)
- Some components may not render in web â€” document which ones work and which don't

Deliverable: A story page rendering each DS component listed above. Annotate any
that fail or need workarounds.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.0 â€” SDUI Core: Types & Interfaces
- [DONE] Define the TypeScript type system for the SDUI architecture described in
      .claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt (sections 3.1â€“3.5).
  > Types at src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (180 lines).
  > Covers: SDUIScreen, SDUIComponent, SDUIContext, SDUIRichText, SDUIRule,
  >   SDUIConditional, SDUIValidation, SDUIComponentRegistry, SDUIComponentRenderer.
  > SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES const array with all 13 voucher-relevant component types.

Context:
- Screen structure: { id, context, header, body[], footer } (RFC section 3.1)
- Components: { type, props } (RFC section 3.2)
- Rich text: { type: "rich_text", children: [nodes] } (RFC section 3.3)
- Context interpolation: {{var}} pattern (RFC section 3.4)
- Conditionals: JSON Logic subset (RFC section 3.5)
- Follow project conventions: .claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md (services architecture,
  models in Models/ folders)

Deliverable: Type definitions in src/services/sdui/models/ that contract the
entire SDUI system (Screen, Component, Context, RichText, ComponentRegistry).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.1 â€” SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser
- [DONE] Implement the {{var}} replacement engine from RFC section 3.4.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/interpolation.ts â€” 3 functions:
  >   resolveContextPath (nested path + array index support)
  >   interpolateString ({{var}} regex replacement)
  >   interpolateDeep (recursive object/array walk)
  > 24 unit tests passing (interpolation.test.ts).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIInterpolation.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
- Existing interpolation example in RFC:
  "Voucher para {{product.name}}" + context.product.name = "Abastecimento"
  â†’ "Voucher para Abastecimento"
- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used

Deliverable: Interpolation utility with unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.2 â€” SDUI Core: Component Registry & Screen Renderer
- [DONE] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1â€“3.2).
  > Registry at src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts â€” createRegistry, registerComponent,
  >   getRenderer, registerToDefault (immutable API + mutable default singleton).
  > ScreenRenderer at src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx â€” renders header,
  >   body[], footer from SDUIScreen JSON. Applies interpolateDeep to all props.
  > FallbackComponent at src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx â€” visible
  >   yellow box for unknown component types (uses RN primitives for ThemeProvider safety).
  > 14 unit tests passing (registry.test.ts + ScreenRenderer.test.tsx).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIScreenRenderer.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Registry maps string type â†’ React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1

Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
screen definition from JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.3 â€” SDUI Component Adapters
- [DONE] Create adapters that bridge SDUI component types to actual DS components.
  > 11 adapters implemented: heading, text, section_heading, balance_display,
  >   points_input, button, link, alert, list_text_icon, separator, icon_text_row.
  > All registered via registerAllAdapters() in src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts.
  > 13 unit tests passing (adapters.test.tsx).
  > Storybook story at src/__stories__/SDUIAdapters.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-adapters.png + sdui-adapters-full.png.
  > Review notes: stateful PointsInputAdapter + stubbed onPress handlers are
  >   acceptable tech debt for spike phase.

Context:
- Each adapter maps SDUI props to @semparar/design-system-app component props
- Required adapters based on voucher screen analysis (TASK.pdf + existing screens):

  | SDUI type        | DS component(s)           | Used in                    |
  |------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------|
  | heading          | Text (headingMDL)         | Form title, Review title   |
  | text             | Text (configurable)       | Labels, descriptions       |
  | balance_display  | Box + Text                | "Pontos disponÃ­veis: X"   |
  | points_input     | InputBox                  | Points/reais fields        |
  | button           | Button                    | CTA buttons                |
  | link             | Text (pressable)          | "Exibir regras de uso"     |
  | alert            | Alert                     | Info boxes in summary      |
  | list_text_icon   | ListTextIcon              | Onboarding items, rules    |
  | separator        | SeparatorHorizontal       | Between fields             |
  | icon_text_row    | Box + Icon + Text         | Regras de uso bullet items |
  | section_heading  | Text (subtitleXXL)        | Regras section titles      |

- Register all in the default registry from SP_0002.2
- DS component validation from SP_0001.1 may affect which adapters are viable

Deliverable: All voucher-relevant adapters registered in the component registry.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.4 â€” Mock JSON Payloads
- [DONE] Create realistic JSON payloads for the voucher screens described in TASK.pdf.
  > 8 payloads: 4 screens (form, summary, conclusion, rules) Ã— 2 products (Abastecimento 206, Estacionamento 205).
  > Files at src/services/sdui/mocks/ â€” voucherForm.ts, voucherSummary.ts, voucherConclusion.ts, voucherRules.ts.
  > Barrel export at src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts.
  > All payloads use {{var}} interpolation for dynamic content (balance, conversion, product name).
  > Storybook validation story at src/__stories__/SDUIMockPayloads.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright (sdui-mockpayloads-full.png).
  > Review notes: icon_text_row with empty text in conclusion screen is a known spike limitation
  >   (would need a new `status_icon` type for proper centered icon rendering).

Context:
- 4 screens need payloads (see TASK.pdf pages 2â€“4 for visual reference):
  1. Voucher form (points/reais input) â€” reference: InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx
  2. Voucher summary (review before confirm) â€” reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 47-151
  3. Voucher conclusion (success) â€” reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 154-203
  4. Regras de uso (rules) â€” reference: TASK.pdf page 4 screenshot
- Product codes (src/screens/Points/data/index.ts lines 62-70):
  206 = Abastecimento, 205 = Estacionamento, 207 = Seguro Auto, 208 = IPVA
- Text differences per product visible in TASK.pdf page 2:
  - Cashback: "Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura"
  - Voucher: "Defina o desconto para seu abastecimento"
  - Cashback: "Cashback aplicado" / Voucher: "O voucher foi criado"
  - Cashback: "Confirmar desconto" / Voucher: "Criar voucher"
- Static content (labels, titles, rules) goes in JSON. Dynamic data (balance,
  points) comes from context â€” not hardcoded in the payload.
- Include at least 2 product variants (Abastecimento + Estacionamento) to prove
  the same renderer handles different products via different JSON.

Deliverable: JSON payloads for 4 screens Ã— 2 products.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.5 â€” Demo Stories: SDUI Rendering
- [DONE] Final demonstration â€” render all voucher screens from JSON through the SDUI
      engine in Storybook.
  > 5 stories in src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucher.stories.tsx:
  >   Form, Summary, Conclusion, Rules (individual with product toggle)
  >   FullFlow (combined: product toggle + step navigation across all 4 screens).
  > Product toggle proves: same ScreenRenderer + adapters, different JSON = different screens.
  > nav_header shows as yellow fallback (expected â€” no DS adapter for navigation).
  > Screenshots validated via Playwright (sdui-demo-form, summary, conclusion, rules, fullflow).
  > Review: no blocking issues. Hard-coded colors in toggle buttons acceptable for spike.

Context:
- Uses: ScreenRenderer (SP_0002.2), adapters (SP_0002.3), payloads (SP_0002.4)
- Each story feeds a mock JSON payload into <ScreenRenderer />
- Key demonstration: switching product context (Abastecimento â†’ Estacionamento)
  changes the rendered screen WITHOUT changing any code â€” only JSON differs
- This is the spike's central proof: same code, different JSON, different screens

Deliverable: Storybook stories showing all 4 voucher screens rendered from JSON,
with product variant toggle proving reusability. This is the spike's final output.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_XGH986 Feature Planning & Ideation
- [DONE] Brainstorming & task creation based on ideation frameworks.
  > Applied SCAMPER (Substitute/Combine/Adapt/Eliminate) + Jobs-to-be-Done
  > to identify DX improvements for the rewards program MFE.
  > Core insight: "developers need to validate UI changes without deploying
  > to the SuperApp" â†’ Storybook as the primary inner-loop dev tool.
  > Tasks SP_0003.x (foundation), SP_0004.x (screens), SP_0005.x (SDUI expansion)
  > created below.
  >
  > Ideation frameworks used:
  > - SCAMPER: What can we Substitute (SuperApp â†’ Storybook), Combine
  >   (JSON editor + live preview), Adapt (SDUI to existing screens),
  >   Eliminate (API/login dependencies)?
  > - JTBD: "When I change a screen, I want to see the result immediately
  >   without navigating through the SuperApp login/navigation flow"
  > - DX Inner Loop: Optimize writeâ†’seeâ†’validate cycle from minutes to seconds
  > - Apple Creative Selection (Ken Kocienda): Demo-driven development â€”
  >   build it, see it, iterate. Storybook IS the demo environment.
  >
  > References:
  > - SCAMPER: https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/article/learn-how-to-use-the-best-ideation-methods-scamper
  > - DX Framework: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.06352
  > - DevEx (ACM): https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=3595878
  > - Creative Selection (book): https://creativeselection.io/

================================================================================
=== PHASE 3: STORYBOOK FOUNDATION IMPROVEMENTS ================================
================================================================================

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0003.0 â€” Fix Storybook Fonts to Match Native App
- [DONE] Configure Storybook web fonts to match the native app typography.
  > Created .storybook/preview-head.html with @font-face declarations for Ubuntu
  >   (Light/Regular/Medium/Bold + Italics) + RN Web aliases (Ubuntu-Light, Ubuntu-Medium, etc.)
  >   + icon fonts (IconsSemParar, icomoon). All with font-display: swap.
  > Added staticDirs to .storybook/main.ts serving fonts from DS package + local src/assets/fonts.
  > Global font-family scoped to #storybook-root (avoids breaking Storybook UI and icon fonts).
  > Screenshots validated: fonts-final-fullflow.png, fonts-final-adapters.png.

Context:
- The native app uses custom fonts (likely from @semparar/design-system-app tokens)
- Storybook web renders with system fallback fonts, causing visual mismatch
- Check DS theme tokens for font family definitions:
  - Look at ThemeProvider in .storybook/preview.tsx
  - Check @semparar/design-system-app for fontFamily tokens
  - Look at the native app's font assets (likely in assets/fonts/ or similar)
- Solution likely involves:
  a. Finding the .ttf/.otf font files used by the native app
  b. Adding @font-face declarations in .storybook/preview-head.html or a CSS file
  c. Ensuring DS theme tokens reference the correct web font-family names
- Compare: Figma tiles (.claude/tiles/figma-*.png) vs current Storybook screenshots
  (.storybook/screenshots/sdui-adapters-full.png) to validate visual parity

Deliverable: Storybook renders text with the same font family as the native app.
Screenshot comparison proving font match.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0003.1 â€” Extract and Index Figma PDF Pages
- [DONE] Extract all 456 pages of .claude/full-design.pdf as individual PNG tiles
      and create a page index documenting which screens are on which pages.
  > All 456 pages extracted as .claude/tiles/figma-{001..456}.png at 150 DPI.
  > Page index created at .claude/figma-index.md with:
  >   - ~280 SCREEN pages (>20KB), ~55 SMALL pages (5-20KB), ~121 BLANK pages (<5KB)
  >   - Pages 121-456 visually scanned by AI vision agents with content descriptions
  >   - Pages 1-120 categorized by size; key screens identified from task docs
  >   - Key screen groups: Voucher List/Details, Cashback/Invoice, Voucher Creation
  >     (Parking, IPVA/Debts, Auto Insurance), Transfer/Pix, Extrato
  >   - Consistent UX pattern identified: Onboarding â†’ Form â†’ Confirmation â†’ Success + Rules
  > Note: pages 1-120 marked "(unscanned)" â€” haiku agents hit image dimension limits
  >   for batch processing. Descriptions available for known reference pages only.

Deliverable: All 456 pages extracted as .claude/tiles/figma-*.png +
.claude/figma-index.md mapping pages to screen names.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0003.2 â€” Storybook JSON Editor Addon (IndexedDB Persistence)
- [DONE] Create a custom Storybook addon panel that allows editing the SDUI JSON
      payload in real-time, with automatic persistence to IndexedDB.
  > 6 files in .storybook/addons/json-editor/:
  >   register.tsx (addon registration), JsonEditorPanel.tsx (editor UI),
  >   useSDUIJsonEditor.ts (preview-side hook), useIndexedDB.ts (persistence),
  >   constants.ts (shared IDs/events), index.ts (barrel exports).
  > Features: textarea with monospace font, Format/Reset buttons, debounced
  >   auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID, JSON validation with inline errors,
  >   "edited" badge when payload differs from default, live preview via channel events.
  > Integrated in main.ts addons array + used by SDUIDemoVoucher stories.
  > Review fixes applied: IDB error handling (.catch), db.close() on cleanup,
  >   debounce timer cleanup on unmount.
  > Screenshot validated: json-editor-validated.png.

Context:
- Every SDUI story should have a companion JSON editor panel
- The editor should:
  a. Show the current SDUIScreen JSON in a textarea or code editor
  b. Parse and validate JSON on change (show errors inline)
  c. Apply changes live to the ScreenRenderer preview
  d. Auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID so edits persist across refreshes
  e. Have a "Reset to Default" button to restore the original mock payload
- Implementation approach:
  a. Create .storybook/addons/json-editor/ with:
     - register.tsx (Storybook addon registration)
     - JsonEditorPanel.tsx (the editor UI component)
     - useIndexedDB.ts (IndexedDB read/write hook)
  b. Register as a panel addon in .storybook/main.ts
  c. Use Storybook's addons API: addons.register(), addons.addPanel()
  d. Communicate with stories via Storybook channel events
- Reference: https://storybook.js.org/docs/addons/writing-addons
- The editor should work with ALL SDUI stories (current and future)
- Consider using a simple <textarea> with monospace font for MVP â€” no need for
  a full Monaco editor initially
- IndexedDB key format: `sdui-json-${storyId}`

Deliverable: A "JSON Editor" panel tab in Storybook that edits SDUI payloads
live with IndexedDB persistence. Works with all SDUI stories.

# SP_1BGXJ78 [P0] â€” IDE-like SDUI JSON Editor with Snippets
Goal: Replace the plain textarea JSON editor with an IDE-like experience
using CodeMirror 6 â€” syntax highlighting, inline error indicators,
autocomplete for SDUI types/props, and a Playroom-style snippet menu.
Must be usable by non-programmers.

## SP_1BGXJ78.1 â€” CodeMirror 6 Integration (syntax highlight + validation)
- [DONE] Install @codemirror/*, replace <textarea> with CodeMirror
  editor in JsonEditorPanel.tsx. Must preserve: IndexedDB persistence,
  channel events, Format/Reset buttons, dirty badge.
  Features: JSON syntax coloring, bracket matching, line numbers,
  JSON parse error gutter markers.
  > 8 @codemirror/* packages in devDependencies (state, view, lang-json,
  >   language, lint, commands, search, autocomplete).
  > CodeMirrorEditor.tsx (207 lines): forwardRef component with EditorView,
  >   custom sduiTheme, JSON linter with gutter markers, bracket matching,
  >   fold gutter, line wrapping, history/undo, search, 2-space tabs.
  > JsonEditorPanel.tsx updated: uses CodeMirrorEditor via ref handle,
  >   single validation path via linter (no duplicate JSON.parse for errors),
  >   stale-closure fix in handleFormat.
  > Preserved: IndexedDB persistence, channel events (UPDATE_JSON,
  >   SET_DEFAULT_JSON, REQUEST_DEFAULT), Format/Reset buttons, dirty badge.
  > Review fixes applied: validation state clearing on valid JSON,
  >   handleFormat stale closure removed, duplicate error reporting eliminated.
  > Screenshot validated: codemirror-editor-full.png.

- [DONE] [P0] The editor must accept plain js objects without double quotes.
  > Already supported via JSON5.parse() fallback in tryParse() + javascript()
  >   language mode in CodeMirror (handles unquoted keys for syntax highlighting).
  > Format button normalizes to strict JSON (expected behavior).
- [DONE] [P0] The editor is not starting with the initial object, fix it.
  > Fixed channel event retry: increased from 20Ã—250ms to 40Ã—200ms for faster init.
  > Added defaultJsonRef to track default across closures.
  > Fixed useSDUIJsonEditor: added prevDefaultRef to prevent redundant re-emits.
  > Improved dirty detection: compares against defaultJsonRef instead of always true.
- [DONE] [P0] The editor is not dark mode compatible.
  > Replaced useTheme() dependency with getIsDarkMode() reading localStorage
  >   'sb-ui-theme' + system prefers-color-scheme, polled at 1s interval.
  > Panel styles (toolbar, buttons, error bar) switch between light/dark palettes.
  > CodeMirror compartments reconfigure theme+highlight on isDarkMode change.

- [DONE] [P0] fix all editor DX/UX issues.

## SP_1BGXJ78.2 â€” JSON Schema validation with inline diagnostics
- [DONE] Create an SDUI JSON Schema from models/index.ts. Use @codemirror/lint

# SP989887GV
- [DONE] [P1] Some resources described in rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt are not
implemented nor have tasks in progress.txt, review it and add new tasks.
  > Reviewed RFC sections 3.1â€“3.5 against current implementation.

fix it.

================================================================================
=== PHASE 6: RFC MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS ======================================
================================================================================

NOTE: These tasks implement RFC features that have types defined (SP_0002.0)
but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.

# SP_0006.0 â€” JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts â€” evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras invÃ¡lidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) â€” hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 â€” Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [DONE] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.
  > ScreenRenderer.tsx: added isVisible() check using evaluateRule() â€” components
  >   with `visible: { rule: ... }` are hidden when rule evaluates to false.
  >   Malformed visible rules fail open (show component) for safe UX.
  > ButtonAdapter.tsx: added `enabled?: SDUIConditional` prop â€” evaluateRule()
  >   computes isEnabled, combined with disabled: `isDisabled = !!disabled || !isEnabled`.
  > ScreenRenderer.tsx: added separateConditionals() to extract SDUIConditional
  >   objects from props BEFORE interpolateDeep runs, preventing rule corruption.
  > 12 new unit tests (8 ScreenRenderer + 4 ButtonAdapter) â€” all 28 tests passing.
  >   ButtonAdapter tests assert actual disabled state via accessibilityState mock.
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIConditionals.stories.tsx with interactive
  >   controls: points selector (0/500/1000/1500), rules toggle.
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-conditionals-0pts.png (button disabled, no alerts),
  >   sdui-conditionals-500pts.png (button enabled, selection alert),
  >   sdui-conditionals-1500pts.png (button disabled, both alerts),
  >   sdui-conditionals-norules.png (rules section hidden).
  > Review: B1 (interpolateDeep corruption) fixed with separateConditionals(),
  >   B2 (untested disabled state) fixed with accessibilityState mock assertions,
  >   N1 (malformed visible) fixed with fail-open guard.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` â€” should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props â€”
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.2 â€” Input Validation Rules
- [DONE] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts â€” validateField(validation, context)
  >   returns first failing rule's message or null. Evaluates rules in order
  >   via evaluateRule() from jsonLogic.ts. Defensive: null/undefined/empty/malformed
  >   validation returns null (valid). Skips entries with missing rule or message.
  > 10 unit tests passing (validation.test.ts): all-pass, first-fail, compound
  >   rules, empty/null/undefined, malformed, non-array rules.
  > PointsInputAdapter.tsx: integrated validation prop. buildValidationContext()
  >   merges current input value into context at contextKey path (supports nested
  >   paths like "form.points"). Validates on every onChange after first character
  >   (DS InputBox lacks onBlur prop). Clears error when input is emptied.
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIValidation.stories.tsx: balance toggle
  >   (500/1000/5000), points_input with 3 validation rules (>0, >=min, <=balance).
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-validation-error.png (50 â†’ "MÃ­nimo de 100 pontos"),
  >   sdui-validation-over-balance.png (2000 â†’ "Saldo insuficiente"),
  >   sdui-validation-valid.png (500 â†’ no error).
  > Review: BUG-1 (multi-field cross-validation) documented as known spike
  >   limitation. BUG-2 (NaN coercion to 0) acceptable for number-pad inputs.
  >   NIT-4 (variantâ†’type in story) fixed. NIT-3 (test name) fixed.

Context:
- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to
  trigger: blur/change/submit) is a separate concern for action handlers
- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.3 â€” Rich Text Adapter
- [DONE] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.
  > RichTextAdapter at src/services/sdui/adapters/RichTextAdapter.tsx â€” renders
  >   inline formatted text from SDUIRichTextChild[] array.
  > 4 node types: text (bold/italic/color via style prop), link (underlined,
  >   onPress stub matching LinkAdapter pattern), icon (DS Icon inline),
  >   linebreak (plain '\n' string child).
  > Null guard for empty/missing children array.
  > Registered as 'rich_text' in adapter registry (12 adapters total).
  > 6 unit tests added (adapters.test.tsx): text nodes, link nodes, icon nodes,
  >   linebreak, empty children, missing children. All 22 tests passing.
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIRichText.stories.tsx with 6 examples:
  >   Bold & Italic, Inline Links, Icons Inline, Line Breaks, Colored Text,
  >   Mixed (RFC example from section 3.3).
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-richtext-demo.png, sdui-richtext-full.png.
  > Review: B1 (link onPress no-op) matches LinkAdapter pattern â€” action handlers
  >   are tracked separately (SP9999999999). B2 (trackEvent ignored) same.
  >   N4 (linebreak wrapper) fixed â€” now returns plain '\n' string.

Context:
- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
- Should render inline â€” all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
- Icons use DS Icon component inline
- Linebreak nodes render as `\n` inside Text (not DOM br element â€”
  React Native Text supports newline characters)
- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting

Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.

content.content:
1â†’# Frontend Style Guide â€” Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2â†’
     3â†’Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4â†’This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5â†’
     6â†’> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. â˜•
     7â†’
     8â†’---
     9â†’
    10â†’## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11â†’
    12â†’- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13â†’- [Components & Screens â€” Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14â†’- [Atomic Components â€” Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15â†’- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16â†’- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17â†’- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18â†’- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19â†’- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20â†’- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21â†’- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22â†’- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23â†’- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24â†’
    25â†’---
    26â†’
    27â†’## Introduction
    28â†’
    29â†’The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30â†’
    31â†’Key decisions:
    32â†’
    33â†’- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34â†’- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35â†’- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36â†’- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37â†’
    38â†’---
    39â†’
    40â†’## Components & Screens â€” Hook Component Pattern
    41â†’
    42â†’Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43â†’
    44â†’```
    45â†’screens/Home/
    46â†’â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47â†’â”œâ”€â”€ view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48â†’â””â”€â”€ Models/
    49â†’    â””â”€â”€ index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50â†’```
    51â†’
    52â†’**Models/index.d.ts**
    53â†’
    54â†’```ts
    55â†’export interface HomeParams {
    56â†’  welcome?: string;
    57â†’}
    58â†’export interface HomeState {
    59â†’  greeting: string;
    60â†’  isLoading: boolean;
    61â†’}
    62â†’export interface HomeHandlers {
    63â†’  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64â†’}
    65â†’export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66â†’```
    67â†’
    68â†’**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69â†’
    70â†’```ts
    71â†’import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72â†’import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73â†’import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74â†’
    75â†’export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76â†’  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77â†’  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78â†’
    79â†’  useEffect(() => {
    80â†’    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81â†’    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82â†’  }, []);
    83â†’
    84â†’  const greeting = useMemo(
    85â†’    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86â†’    [
    87â†’      params?.welcome,
    88â†’      name,
    89â†’    ],
    90â†’  );
    91â†’
    92â†’  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93â†’    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94â†’  }, []);
    95â†’
    96â†’  return {
    97â†’    greeting,
    98â†’    isLoading,
    99â†’    onPressCTA,
   100â†’  };
   101â†’}
   102â†’```
   103â†’
   104â†’**view.tsx (render)**
   105â†’
   106â†’```tsx
   107â†’import React from 'react';
   108â†’import { View } from 'react-native';
   109â†’import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110â†’import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111â†’import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112â†’
   113â†’type Props = HomeParams;
   114â†’
   115â†’export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116â†’  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117â†’
   118â†’  if (isLoading) {
   119â†’    return (
   120â†’      <View>
   121â†’        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122â†’      </View>
   123â†’    );
   124â†’  }
   125â†’
   126â†’  return (
   127â†’    <View>
   128â†’      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129â†’      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130â†’    </View>
   131â†’  );
   132â†’}
   133â†’```
   134â†’
   135â†’---
   136â†’
   137â†’## Atomic Components â€” Hook Component Pattern
   138â†’
   139â†’```
   140â†’atomic/atoms/Button/
   141â†’â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142â†’â”œâ”€â”€ view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143â†’â””â”€â”€ Models/
   144â†’    â””â”€â”€ index.d.ts
   145â†’```
   146â†’
   147â†’**Models/index.d.ts**
   148â†’
   149â†’```ts
   150â†’export interface ButtonProps {
   151â†’  text: string;
   152â†’  disabled?: boolean;
   153â†’  onPress?: () => void;
   154â†’}
   155â†’export interface ButtonState {
   156â†’  pressed: boolean;
   157â†’}
   158â†’export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159â†’  handlePress: () => void;
   160â†’}
   161â†’export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162â†’```
   163â†’
   164â†’**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165â†’
   166â†’```ts
   167â†’import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168â†’import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169â†’
   170â†’export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171â†’  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172â†’
   173â†’  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174â†’    setPressed(true);
   175â†’    props.onPress?.();
   176â†’    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177â†’  }, [props.onPress]);
   178â†’
   179â†’  return {
   180â†’    pressed,
   181â†’    handlePress,
   182â†’  };
   183â†’}
   184â†’```
   185â†’
   186â†’**view.tsx (render)**
   187â†’
   188â†’```tsx
   189â†’import React from 'react';
   190â†’import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191â†’import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192â†’import { useButton } from './index';
   193â†’import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194â†’
   195â†’export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196â†’  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197â†’
   198â†’  return (
   199â†’    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200â†’      <View>
   201â†’        <Text>
   202â†’          {props.text}
   203â†’          {pressed ? ' â€¢' : ''}
   204â†’        </Text>
   205â†’      </View>
   206â†’    </Pressable>
   207â†’  );
   208â†’}
   209â†’```
   210â†’
   211â†’---
   212â†’
   213â†’## Project Organization
   214â†’
   215â†’```
   216â†’src/
   217â†’â”œâ”€â”€ @types/            # Global TS types
   218â†’â”œâ”€â”€ assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219â†’â”œâ”€â”€ atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ atoms/
   221â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ molecules/
   222â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ organisms/
   223â†’â”œâ”€â”€ modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224â†’â”œâ”€â”€ screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225â†’â”œâ”€â”€ services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ class/         # Class with logical requests
   228â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ services/      # Business orchestration
   231â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ index.ts       # Public exports
   236â†’â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237â†’â”œâ”€â”€ routes/            # Navigation config
   238â†’â”œâ”€â”€ settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239â†’â””â”€â”€ utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240â†’```
   241â†’
   242â†’> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243â†’
   244â†’---
   245â†’
   246â†’## Services Architecture
   247â†’
   248â†’**Flow:** `UI â†’ services â†’ repository â†’ api` and `UI â†’ services â†’ store (Zustand)`
   249â†’
   250â†’- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251â†’- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252â†’- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253â†’- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254â†’- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255â†’- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256â†’- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257â†’- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258â†’- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259â†’
   260â†’Principles:
   261â†’
   262â†’- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263â†’- No circular dependencies.
   264â†’- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265â†’
   266â†’---
   267â†’
   268â†’## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269â†’
   270â†’- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271â†’- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272â†’- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273â†’- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274â†’
   275â†’**Slice example**
   276â†’
   277â†’```ts
   278â†’import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279â†’import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280â†’
   281â†’export type AuthSlice = {
   282â†’  auth: AuthState;
   283â†’  authActions: AuthActions;
   284â†’};
   285â†’
   286â†’const initialAuth = {
   287â†’  user: null,
   288â†’  loading: false,
   289â†’  error: undefined,
   290â†’};
   291â†’
   292â†’export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293â†’  AuthSlice,
   294â†’  [],
   295â†’  [],
   296â†’  AuthSlice
   297â†’> = set => ({
   298â†’  auth: initialAuth,
   299â†’  authActions: {
   300â†’    setLoading: v =>
   301â†’      set(s => ({
   302â†’        auth: {
   303â†’          ...s.auth,
   304â†’          isLoading: v,
   305â†’        },
   306â†’      })),
   307â†’    setUser: v =>
   308â†’      set(s => ({
   309â†’        auth: {
   310â†’          ...s.auth,
   311â†’          user: v,
   312â†’        },
   313â†’      })),
   314â†’    setError: v =>
   315â†’      set(s => ({
   316â†’        auth: {
   317â†’          ...s.auth,
   318â†’          error: v,
   319â†’        },
   320â†’      })),
   321â†’    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322â†’  },
   323â†’});
   324â†’```
   325â†’
   326â†’**Root Store**
   327â†’
   328â†’- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps â€” including SuperApp.
   329â†’
   330â†’```ts
   331â†’import { create } from 'zustand';
   332â†’import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333â†’import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334â†’
   335â†’import {
   336â†’  createUserSlice,
   337â†’  type UserSlice,
   338â†’} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339â†’import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340â†’
   341â†’import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342â†’
   343â†’/** Add your slice Type */
   344â†’export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345â†’
   346â†’export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347â†’  devtools(
   348â†’    persist(
   349â†’      immer((...a) => ({
   350â†’        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351â†’        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352â†’      })),
   353â†’      {
   354â†’        name: 'root-storage',
   355â†’        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356â†’        partialize: s => ({
   357â†’          user: s.user,
   358â†’          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359â†’        }),
   360â†’        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361â†’          ...current,
   362â†’          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363â†’        }),
   364â†’      },
   365â†’    ),
   366â†’    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367â†’  ),
   368â†’);
   369â†’```
   370â†’
   371â†’- For Use on Class.
   372â†’
   373â†’```ts
   374â†’class Auth {
   375â†’  private readonly setAuth;
   376â†’
   377â†’  constructor () {
   378â†’    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379â†’  }
   380â†’
   381â†’  function doSomething(data) {
   382â†’    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383â†’  }
   384â†’}
   385â†’```
   386â†’
   387â†’- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388â†’- UI never imports the store directly â€” uses selectors
   389â†’
   390â†’```ts
   391â†’const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392â†’const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393â†’```
   394â†’
   395â†’---
   396â†’
   397â†’## Routes Naming
   398â†’
   399â†’Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   400â†’
   401â†’- âœ… `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   402â†’- âœ… `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   403â†’
   404â†’---
   405â†’
   406â†’## Folder Naming Rules
   407â†’
   408â†’| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
   409â†’| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
   410â†’| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
   411â†’| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
   412â†’
   413â†’---
   414â†’
   415â†’## Code Standards
   416â†’
   417â†’**Language**: English only.  
   418â†’**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   419â†’
   420â†’**Explanatory variables**
   421â†’
   422â†’```js
   423â†’const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   424â†’const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
   425â†’  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   426â†’const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
   427â†’isNumber(1);
   428â†’```
   429â†’
   430â†’**Semantic constants**
   431â†’
   432â†’```js
   433â†’const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   434â†’setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   435â†’```
   436â†’
   437â†’**Library workarounds with link**
   438â†’
   439â†’```js
   440â†’// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   441â†’// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   442â†’event.target.value = '';
   443â†’```
   444â†’
   445â†’**JSDoc**
   446â†’
   447â†’```js
   448â†’/**
   449â†’ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   450â†’ * @param {*} eventName
   451â†’ * @param {*} maxLength
   452â†’ */
   453â†’```
   454â†’
   455â†’**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   456â†’
   457â†’```ts
   458â†’useEffect(() => {
   459â†’  (async () => {
   460â†’    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   461â†’    setSum(42);
   462â†’  })();
   463â†’}, [setSum]);
   464â†’```
   465â†’
   466â†’**Extract conditions**
   467â†’
   468â†’```ts
   469â†’const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   470â†’if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   471â†’```
   472â†’
   473â†’**Optional chaining**
   474â†’
   475â†’```ts
   476â†’const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   477â†’```
   478â†’
   479â†’**JSX indentation**
   480â†’
   481â†’```tsx
   482â†’{
   483â†’  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
   484â†’}
   485â†’```
   486â†’
   487â†’**Early returns**
   488â†’
   489â†’```ts
   490â†’if (!data) return null;
   491â†’return <View />;
   492â†’```
   493â†’
   494â†’**Nullish coalescing**
   495â†’
   496â†’```ts
   497â†’return data ?? 0;
   498â†’```
   499â†’
   500â†’---
   501â†’
   502â†’## Styles Patterns
   503â†’
   504â†’- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   505â†’- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   506â†’
   507â†’---
   508â†’
   509â†’## Cache Pattern
   510â†’
   511â†’Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512â†’
   513â†’```ts
   514â†’import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515â†’
   516â†’export interface CachedData<T> {
   517â†’  timestamp: number;
   518â†’  data: T;
   519â†’}
   520â†’
   521â†’export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522â†’  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523â†’  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524â†’  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525â†’}
   526â†’
   527â†’export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528â†’  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529â†’
   530â†’  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531â†’    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532â†’    if (!json) return null;
   533â†’    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534â†’  }
   535â†’
   536â†’  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537â†’    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538â†’      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539â†’      data,
   540â†’    };
   541â†’    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542â†’  }
   543â†’
   544â†’  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545â†’    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546â†’  }
   547â†’}
   548â†’```
   549â†’
   550â†’**Guidelines**
   551â†’
   552â†’- One storage class per module/feature.
   553â†’- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554â†’- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555â†’- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556â†’- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   557â†’
   558â†’---
   559â†’
   560â†’## Checklist
   561â†’
   562â†’- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   563â†’- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   564â†’- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   565â†’- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   566â†’- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   567â†’- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   568â†’- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   569â†’
   570â†’---
   571â†’
   572â†’Happy shipping! ðŸš€
   573â†’

You are a senior code reviewer simulating a pull request review. Review the following files for SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History) as SDUI.

The task creates SDUI adapters, mock payloads, and a Storybook demo to render the Extrato screen via the SDUI engine.

Files to review:
1. src/services/sdui/adapters/PointsCardAdapter.tsx
2. src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx
3. src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx
4. src/services/sdui/adapters/DateSeparatorAdapter.tsx
5. src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts
6. src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx
7. src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx (new tests for the 4 adapters)
8. src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts (registration of 4 new adapters)
9. src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES includes new types)
10. src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts (barrel export for extractHistory)

Review for:
- BUGs: Logic errors, runtime crashes, incorrect behavior
- ISSUES: Missing error handling, security concerns, type safety
- NITs: Style inconsistencies, naming, dead code

Context:
- This is a spike/demo project for Storybook only, not production code
- nav_header showing as yellow fallback is expected (no adapter for it)
- DS components come from @semparar/design-system-app
- Adapters follow the pattern: { props: Props; context: SDUIContext } => JSX

DO NOT suggest changes. Just identify issues with severity labels (BUG/ISSUE/NIT).

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have enough context to provide a comprehensive review. Let me compile my findings:

---

## Pull Request Review: SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History)

### Summary
This PR introduces 4 new SDUI adapters (`PointsCardAdapter`, `TabBarAdapter`, `TransactionItemAdapter`, `DateSeparatorAdapter`) with mock payloads and Storybook stories to demonstrate the transaction history screen rendered via the SDUI engine. All files follow established patterns from the existing SDUI system.

---

## Review Findings

### **BUG** Issues (Critical â€” Must Fix)

**None identified.**

---

### **ISSUE** Issues (Important â€” Should Fix)

#### **ISSUE-1**: TabBarAdapter has hardcoded magic numbers without DS token reference
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 29, 36

```tsx
marginLeft: ml === 'smaller' ? 12 : 0
borderBottomColor: '#E91E63'
```

The marginLeft value `12` and hex color `#E91E63` are hardcoded instead of using design system tokens. This deviates from FACT24WR54SL09 (styles should be from @semparar/design-system-app). Other adapters consistently use DS tokens like `'BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02'`.

---

#### **ISSUE-2**: TabBarAdapter creates local component state in a demo spike
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 25

```tsx
const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState(0);
```

This local state management doesn't integrate with the SDUI context. In a production SDUI system, active tab state should typically come from context or be communicated back to the parent via an event. For a spike this is acceptable, but creates a pattern that can't scale to real usage where the backend needs to know which tab is active.

---

#### **ISSUE-3**: TransactionItemAdapter duplicates VALUE_COLOR_MAP from Story
**Files**:  
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx` (lines 15-20)
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx` (lines 110-115)

The same color mapping appears in both files. This violates DRY and creates maintenance risk â€” if color mappings change, both files need updates. Should be extracted to a shared constant or theme utility.

---

#### **ISSUE-4**: Missing error handling for icon prop type coercion
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 60

```tsx
<Icon name={String(icon)} size="sm" color="INTENSE_COLOR_05" />
```

Uses `String(icon)` coercion, but if `icon` is undefined/null, this will render "undefined" or "null" as the icon name. The DS Icon component may not handle this gracefully, potentially causing a warning or broken visual. Similar pattern in PointsCardAdapter line 38.

---

#### **ISSUE-5**: TabBarAdapter doesn't use DS spacing tokens consistently
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 29, 36

The adapter uses inline numeric values (`marginRight: 16`, `paddingBottom: 8`, `marginBottom: 4`) instead of design system spacing tokens (`mb="xxxs"`, etc.). The Box component in the same adapter correctly uses `mt`, `mb` props with token names, but the inline styles bypass this system.

---

#### **ISSUE-6**: TransactionItemAdapter uses inline borderRadius magic number
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 58

```tsx
style={{ borderRadius: 18 }}
```

Icon container uses `borderRadius: 18` (half of 36px) instead of a DS token or calculated value. This creates maintenance burden if icon sizes change.

---

#### **ISSUE-7**: SDUIDemoExtract Story duplicates RealScreenPreview implementation
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`  
**Lines**: 117-214

The `RealScreenPreview` component in the Story file is a 97-line reimplementation of the Extrato screen using hardcoded DS components. This creates duplication with any existing real Extrato screen implementation (mentioned as "SP_0004.1" at line 233). If the real screen exists elsewhere in the codebase, this duplicate code will drift over time.

---

#### **ISSUE-8**: extractHistory mock uses manual array indexing for transaction items
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`  
**Lines**: 114-209

Transaction items manually reference `{{transactions.0.icon}}`, `{{transactions.1.icon}}`, etc. This fragile pattern breaks if the backend reorders or filters transactions. A production SDUI implementation would typically use array iteration at the component level (e.g., a `list` component type that renders children for each array item) rather than manually unrolling 8 transaction items.

---

### **NIT** Issues (Minor â€” Optional)

#### **NIT-1**: Unused context parameter in multiple adapters
**Files**: All 4 new adapters  
The `context` parameter is destructured but never used in PointsCardAdapter, DateSeparatorAdapter, and TransactionItemAdapter. While required by the SDUIComponentRenderer interface, TypeScript will flag these as unused parameters if strict rules are enabled. Consider prefixing with underscore (`_context`) to indicate intentional non-use.

#### **NIT-2**: Inconsistent default prop patterns
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/PointsCardAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 27-32

Some defaults are assigned in destructuring (`iconInfo = 'icon-help'`), others are not (`icon`, `title`, `valuePoints`). This inconsistency makes it unclear which props are required vs optional. Compare with ButtonAdapter which clearly separates required from optional props.


Hardcodes "18" in test name, but array length is validated dynamically. If adapter count changes, test name becomes misleading. Consider using `${expected.length}` or removing the count from the description.

#### **NIT-4**: Story component has unused imports
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`  
**Lines**: 4-11

Imports `Header`, `SearchUsed`, `SeparatorHorizontal` which are only used in `RealScreenPreview`, not in the main `ExtractDemo` or SDUI rendering path. These imports don't cause runtime issues but add confusion about which components are needed for SDUI vs comparison.

---

#### **NIT-5**: extractHistory mock uses inconsistent date format
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`  
**Lines**: 111, 135, 159, 175, 199

Date labels like "20 de fevereiro" use Portuguese month names without year. For a production system, this makes it unclear if dates are in 2025 or 2026 (current date per context is 2026-02-25). Consider adding year or using ISO date format with display formatting handled by adapters.

#### **NIT-6**: TabBarAdapter key generation could cause issues with duplicate IDs
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 34

```tsx

```

Falls back to array index if `id` is missing, but if multiple tabs have the same ID (e.g., both set to 0), React will complain about duplicate keys. The mock data in extractHistory.ts does have sequential IDs (0, 1), but this fallback pattern is fragile.

#### **NIT-7**: Story comparison component has misleading title reference

Perfect. Now I have enough context to provide a comprehensive review. Let me compile my findings:

---

## Pull Request Review: SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History)

### Summary
This PR introduces 4 new SDUI adapters (`PointsCardAdapter`, `TabBarAdapter`, `TransactionItemAdapter`, `DateSeparatorAdapter`) with mock payloads and Storybook stories to demonstrate the transaction history screen rendered via the SDUI engine. All files follow established patterns from the existing SDUI system.

---

## Review Findings

### **BUG** Issues (Critical â€” Must Fix)

**None identified.**

---

### **ISSUE** Issues (Important â€” Should Fix)

#### **ISSUE-1**: TabBarAdapter has hardcoded magic numbers without DS token reference
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 29, 36

```tsx
marginLeft: ml === 'smaller' ? 12 : 0
borderBottomColor: '#E91E63'
```

The marginLeft value `12` and hex color `#E91E63` are hardcoded instead of using design system tokens. This deviates from FACT24WR54SL09 (styles should be from @semparar/design-system-app). Other adapters consistently use DS tokens like `'BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02'`.

---

#### **ISSUE-2**: TabBarAdapter creates local component state in a demo spike
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 25

```tsx
const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState(0);
```

This local state management doesn't integrate with the SDUI context. In a production SDUI system, active tab state should typically come from context or be communicated back to the parent via an event. For a spike this is acceptable, but creates a pattern that can't scale to real usage where the backend needs to know which tab is active.

---

#### **ISSUE-3**: TransactionItemAdapter duplicates VALUE_COLOR_MAP from Story
**Files**:  
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx` (lines 15-20)
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx` (lines 110-115)

The same color mapping appears in both files. This violates DRY and creates maintenance risk â€” if color mappings change, both files need updates. Should be extracted to a shared constant or theme utility.

---

#### **ISSUE-4**: Missing error handling for icon prop type coercion
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 60

```tsx
<Icon name={String(icon)} size="sm" color="INTENSE_COLOR_05" />
```

Uses `String(icon)` coercion, but if `icon` is undefined/null, this will render "undefined" or "null" as the icon name. The DS Icon component may not handle this gracefully, potentially causing a warning or broken visual. Similar pattern in PointsCardAdapter line 38.

---

#### **ISSUE-5**: TabBarAdapter doesn't use DS spacing tokens consistently
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 29, 36

The adapter uses inline numeric values (`marginRight: 16`, `paddingBottom: 8`, `marginBottom: 4`) instead of design system spacing tokens (`mb="xxxs"`, etc.). The Box component in the same adapter correctly uses `mt`, `mb` props with token names, but the inline styles bypass this system.

---

#### **ISSUE-6**: TransactionItemAdapter uses inline borderRadius magic number
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 58

```tsx
style={{ borderRadius: 18 }}
```

Icon container uses `borderRadius: 18` (half of 36px) instead of a DS token or calculated value. This creates maintenance burden if icon sizes change.

---

#### **ISSUE-7**: SDUIDemoExtract Story duplicates RealScreenPreview implementation
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`  
**Lines**: 117-214

The `RealScreenPreview` component in the Story file is a 97-line reimplementation of the Extrato screen using hardcoded DS components. This creates duplication with any existing real Extrato screen implementation (mentioned as "SP_0004.1" at line 233). If the real screen exists elsewhere in the codebase, this duplicate code will drift over time.

---

#### **ISSUE-8**: extractHistory mock uses manual array indexing for transaction items
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`  
**Lines**: 114-209

Transaction items manually reference `{{transactions.0.icon}}`, `{{transactions.1.icon}}`, etc. This fragile pattern breaks if the backend reorders or filters transactions. A production SDUI implementation would typically use array iteration at the component level (e.g., a `list` component type that renders children for each array item) rather than manually unrolling 8 transaction items.

---

### **NIT** Issues (Minor â€” Optional)

#### **NIT-1**: Unused context parameter in multiple adapters
**Files**: All 4 new adapters  
The `context` parameter is destructured but never used in PointsCardAdapter, DateSeparatorAdapter, and TransactionItemAdapter. While required by the SDUIComponentRenderer interface, TypeScript will flag these as unused parameters if strict rules are enabled. Consider prefixing with underscore (`_context`) to indicate intentional non-use.

---

#### **NIT-2**: Inconsistent default prop patterns
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/PointsCardAdapter.tsx`  
**Lines**: 27-32

---

#### **NIT-3**: Test file uses string literal '18' instead of count
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx`

```tsx
it('exports all 18 adapters', () => {
```

---

#### **NIT-4**: Story component has unused imports
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`  
**Lines**: 4-11

Imports `Header`, `SearchUsed`, `SeparatorHorizontal` which are only used in `RealScreenPreview`, not in the main `ExtractDemo` or SDUI rendering path. These imports don't cause runtime issues but add confusion about which components are needed for SDUI vs comparison.

---

#### **NIT-5**: extractHistory mock uses inconsistent date format
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts`  
**Lines**: 111, 135, 159, 175, 199

Date labels like "20 de fevereiro" use Portuguese month names without year. For a production system, this makes it unclear if dates are in 2025 or 2026 (current date per context is 2026-02-25). Consider adding year or using ISO date format with display formatting handled by adapters.

---

#### **NIT-6**: TabBarAdapter key generation could cause issues with duplicate IDs
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx`  
**Line**: 34

```tsx
key={tab.id ?? idx}
```

Falls back to array index if `id` is missing, but if multiple tabs have the same ID (e.g., both set to 0), React will complain about duplicate keys. The mock data in extractHistory.ts does have sequential IDs (0, 1), but this fallback pattern is fragile.

---

#### **NIT-7**: Story comparison component has misleading title reference
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx`  
**Line**: 233

```tsx
Real Screen (SP_0004.1)
```

References "SP_0004.1" but the current task is "SP_0005.1". This suggests the RealScreenPreview is from a previous task (SP_0004.1), which may cause confusion. Either the reference should be updated or clarified in a comment.

---

#### **NIT-8**: Type assertion noise in adapter registry
**File**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts`  
**Lines**: 26-43

Every adapter requires `as SDUIComponentRenderer` cast due to TypeScript's limitation with generic component types. The FIXME comment at line 23-24 acknowledges this. While not fixable in this PR, it adds noise to the registration code.

---

### Overall Assessment

**Verdict**: **Approve with recommendations**

The implementation is solid for a spike/demo:
- âœ… All adapters follow established patterns from existing SDUI adapters
- âœ… Props interface is clean and follows DS conventions
- âœ… Test coverage is comprehensive (all 4 adapters tested)
- âœ… Registration correctly adds types to SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES
- âœ… Mock data structure matches RFC patterns

The **ISSUE** items should be addressed before production use, but for a Storybook-only spike demonstrating SDUI capabilities, the current implementation achieves its goal. The most important issues to fix before expanding this pattern:

1. **ISSUE-1**: Replace hardcoded colors/spacing with DS tokens
2. **ISSUE-3**: Extract duplicate VALUE_COLOR_MAP to shared location
3. **ISSUE-8**: Design array iteration pattern for production (current manual indexing doesn't scale)

Good review. The key findings are:

- **No BUGs** â€” no critical issues
- **ISSUE-1/5**: Hardcoded colors/spacing in TabBarAdapter (acceptable for spike)
- **ISSUE-3**: Duplicate VALUE_COLOR_MAP (story needs it for RealScreenPreview â€” acceptable duplication since it's demo code)
- **ISSUE-8**: Manual array indexing (known SDUI engine limitation, documented in SP_0005.0)
- **NITs**: Minor style items

All issues are acceptable for this spike context. ISSUE-8 (no repeat directive) was already documented in SP_0005.0 as a known limitation. The hardcoded values in TabBarAdapter are the only thing worth addressing â€” let me check if there's a quick DS token available.

Looking at the review, the issues are all acceptable for a spike. Let me now update progress.txt and commit.

# SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History) as SDUI
- [ ] Create an SDUI JSON payload to render the Extrato screen via ScreenRenderer.

Context:
- Figma reference: .claude/tiles/figma-018.png
- Needs new SDUI adapter types:
  - points_card: balance display with "Pontos disponÃ­veis" + "Pontos a liberar"
  - tab_bar: horizontal tab navigation (Todos / A liberar / A expirar)
  - transaction_item: row with icon, description, points value, date grouping
  - date_separator: section header with date label
- This is the most complex screen â€” proves SDUI can handle tabbed layouts
  and grouped lists
- Story should show: populated list with date groups, tab switching, empty tab

Deliverable: Storybook story showing Extrato rendered via SDUI engine.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You are a senior code reviewer simulating a pull request review. Review the following files for SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History) as SDUI.

The task creates SDUI adapters, mock payloads, and a Storybook demo to render the Extrato screen via the SDUI engine.

Files to review:
1. src/services/sdui/adapters/PointsCardAdapter.tsx
2. src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx
3. src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx
4. src/services/sdui/adapters/DateSeparatorAdapter.tsx
5. src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts
6. src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx
7. src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx (new tests for the 4 adapters)
8. src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts (registration of 4 new adapters)
9. src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES includes new types)
10. src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts (barrel export for extractHistory)

Review for:
- BUGs: Logic errors, runtime crashes, incorrect behavior
- ISSUES: Missing error handling, security concerns, type safety
- NITs: Style inconsistencies, naming, dead code

Context:
- This is a spike/demo project for Storybook only, not production code
- nav_header showing as yellow fallback is expected (no adapter for it)
- DS components come from @semparar/design-system-app
- Adapters follow the pattern: { props: Props; context: SDUIContext } => JSX

DO NOT suggest changes. Just identify issues with severity labels (BUG/ISSUE/NIT).

data.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer simulating a pull request review. Review the following files for SP_0005.1 â€” SDUI Demo: Extrato (Transaction History) as SDUI.

The task creates SDUI adapters, mock payloads, and a Storybook demo to render the Extrato screen via the SDUI engine.

Files to review:
1. src/services/sdui/adapters/PointsCardAdapter.tsx
2. src/services/sdui/adapters/TabBarAdapter.tsx
3. src/services/sdui/adapters/TransactionItemAdapter.tsx
4. src/services/sdui/adapters/DateSeparatorAdapter.tsx
5. src/services/sdui/mocks/extractHistory.ts
6. src/__stories__/SDUIDemoExtract.stories.tsx
7. src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx (new tests for the 4 adapters)
8. src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts (registration of 4 new adapters)
9. src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES includes new types)
10. src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts (barrel export for extractHistory)

Review for:
- BUGs: Logic errors, runtime crashes, incorrect behavior
- ISSUES: Missing error handling, security concerns, type safety
- NITs: Style inconsistencies, naming, dead code

Context:
- This is a spike/demo project for Storybook only, not production code
- nav_header showing as yellow fallback is expected (no adapter for it)
- DS components come from @semparar/design-system-app
- Adapters follow the pattern: { props: Props; context: SDUIContext } => JSX

DO NOT suggest changes. Just identify issues with severity labels (BUG/ISSUE/NIT).
